context: Tests whether the agent creates a key validation module that uses the correct ElevenLabs API endpoint, correct authentication header, and stores keys in the correct location with the correct variable name.

checklist.name: Validation endpoint
checklist.description: The validateKey function makes a request to https://api.elevenlabs.io/v1/user (the /v1/user path specifically)

checklist.name: Auth header xi-api-key
checklist.description: The HTTP request uses 'xi-api-key' as the header name (not 'Authorization' with Bearer, not 'x-api-key', not 'apikey')

checklist.name: GET method
checklist.description: The validation request uses the HTTP GET method (not POST, PUT, or other)

checklist.name: Saves to .env file
checklist.description: The saveKey function writes the key to a file named '.env' (not a JSON config file, not environment variables directly)

checklist.name: Uses ELEVENLABS_API_KEY
checklist.description: The key is stored with the variable name ELEVENLABS_API_KEY in the .env file

checklist.name: Dotenv format
checklist.description: The .env file uses standard dotenv format: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY=<value> (one variable per line, no JSON, no YAML)

checklist.name: loadKey reads .env
checklist.description: The loadKey function reads from the .env file to retrieve a previously stored key

checklist.name: Returns boolean or status
checklist.description: The validateKey function returns a boolean indicating success/failure (or a promise resolving to boolean), distinguishing valid from invalid keys

checklist.name: Handles HTTP errors
checklist.description: The validateKey function checks the HTTP response status to determine if the key is valid (e.g., 200 = valid, 401 = invalid)

checklist.name: Module exports all three
checklist.description: The module exports validateKey, saveKey, and loadKey as named exports (all three functions are accessible from the module)
